ContactSubmission.title: ContactSubmission
ContactSubmission.description: Represents a contact message submitted by a user through the website's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactSubmission entity.

name.description: The name provided by the user submitting the contact form.

email.description: The email address provided by the user for contact.

message.description: The message content submitted by the user. (Approximately 300 words limit, represented by maxLength of 2000 characters).

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the contact message was submitted.

definition.description: Stores contact messages submitted by users through the website's contact form. Any user (authenticated or anonymous) can create a new submission. Only authorized administrators can read these submissions, with authorization managed via a separate global roles collection. No denormalization is needed within this document for authorization as access is based on global roles.

params.name: contactSubmissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific contact message submission.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for `ContactSubmission` entities is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and adherence to QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies). All `ContactSubmission` documents will reside in a top-level collection named `/contact_submissions`. This collection follows the 'Structural Segregation' mandate by ensuring all documents within it share a homogeneous security posture: any user (authenticated or anonymous) can create a new submission, while only designated administrators can read existing submissions.

Authorization Independence is achieved for read access by separating the definition of administrative roles from the `ContactSubmission` documents themselves. Instead of relying on data within the `ContactSubmission` document (or its non-existent parent) for authorization, administrative access will be determined by the presence of the `request.auth.uid` in a dedicated 'global roles' collection (e.g., `/app_roles/admin_users/{uid}`). This adheres to the DBAC principle and eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules to check for administrative privileges, allowing for atomic operations and simpler debugging. For creation, `request.auth.uid` is not required, allowing anonymous submissions.

QAPs are fully supported. For 'create' operations on `/contact_submissions`, rules will permit any user to write, as there's no pre-filtering required based on the user's identity or document content. For 'list' and 'get' operations, administrators can securely query `/contact_submissions` directly. The security rule for reading would check if `request.auth.uid` exists in the `/app_roles/admin_users` collection. This allows an admin to fetch all submissions efficiently without the rules acting as data filters, thus enabling efficient and secure list operations. The `ContactSubmission` document itself contains all necessary information (`name`, `email`, `message`, `submissionDate`) without relying on external lookups for its core data or authorization for its creation.
